Meaning.En: A structure built along the shore of a harbor, river, or canal where ships can dock and load or unload cargo and passengers.
Meaning.Example: The cruise ship pulled into the dock just before sunset.

Situation.Vi: Hình ảnh tàu thủy neo đậu tại bến để hành khách lên xuống hoặc hàng hóa được bốc dỡ.
Situation.En: An image of a ship anchored at a pier for passenger embarkation/disembarkation or cargo handling.

Meaning.Vi: Bến đậu, neo tàu; đưa tàu vào bến
Meaning.En: To bring a ship or boat alongside a dock or pier and secure it there.
Meaning.Example: The captain ordered the crew to dock the vessel at Pier 3.

Situation.Vi: Hành động tàu cập cảng và buộc dây vào cọc hoặc bến để ổn định.
Situation.En: The action of a vessel approaching and tying up at a dock to remain stationary.

Meaning.En: To confine someone, especially a suspect or prisoner, in a holding cell or jail.
Meaning.Example: The police docked the suspect overnight before arraignment.

Situation.Vi: Cảnh sát giữ nghi phạm trong buồng giam tạm thời.
Situation.En: Police temporarily holding a suspect in a detention cell before formal charges.

Meaning.Vi: Giảm (tiền lương, quyền lợi) do vi phạm
Meaning.En: To reduce someone’s pay or benefits as a penalty for misconduct.
Meaning.Example: The manager docked his pay for arriving late three times in one week.

Situation.Vi: Nhà quản lý trừ lương nhân viên vì vi phạm quy định.
Situation.En: An employer deducting wages from an employee’s paycheck as disciplinary action.
